title: Boat Rental in Athens: Tips, Prices and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Athens lets you explore the Saronic Gulf from Alimos Marina, with shared tours from €60 per person or a private motorboat from €250 for a half-day through BoatHire24. The coastline rewards those who set off early, with calm waters and island views that stretch to Aegina.

Where to set off in Athens

Most charters depart from Alimos Marina, the largest in Athens, followed by Flisvos Marina in Paleo Faliro and the port of Piraeus. The nearby bay of Vouliagmeni offers shelter for a swim stop, while the islands of Hydra, Spetses and Poros make popular day trips. Each departure point has its own character and proximity to open water.

Which boat to rent

  1. Rib tender Best for a quick run to a beach taverna, seating up to 4, from €150 for a half-day.
  2. Motorboat Ideal for a family day out with 6 guests, from €350 for a full-day, including the Olympic 490 listing.
  3. Sailing yacht Suitable for a relaxed cruise with 8 people, from €550 for a full-day.
  4. Catamaran Spacious for up to 12 guests, from €800 for a full-day, great for groups.
  5. Crewed motor yacht Luxury option with skipper, for up to 10 guests, from €1,200 for a full-day, brands include Azimut and Sunseeker.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Athens?

Prices range from €60 per person for a shared tour to €1,200 for a crewed yacht full-day charter in 2026. The cost depends on boat size, season and duration. Here is a typical breakdown:

  • Shared sunset tour: from €60 per person, 2 hours, groups of 10, 12.
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €250 for a 4-hour rental, fuel included.
  • Catamaran full-day: from €800 for 8 hours, skipper optional.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: from €1,200, includes crew, fuel and snacks.

For the best deals on verified boats, check yacht charter Athens options on BoatHire24, where you can compare prices and book online.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Athens?

Yes, for bareboat motorboats over 30hp a licence is required, but skippered charters are licence-free. Greek law requires a valid boat licence for self-drive, and the operator will confirm the exact requirements before booking. Most visitors opt for a skipper to avoid paperwork and enjoy the views.

Local tip: book a skipper for your first day; they know the best coves around Aegina and can handle the Meltemi wind that picks up in July and August.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €1,200+ per person or boat
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full-day
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on most boats
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Athens

Can I rent a boat in Athens without a licence?

Yes, you can hire a skippered boat without any licence, which is the most common choice for tourists.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Athens?

The best time is between May and October, with July and August offering the hottest weather and the strongest Meltemi wind.

How far is it from Athens to Aegina by boat?

The distance from Alimos Marina to Aegina town is about 16 nautical miles, taking roughly 90 minutes in a motorboat.

Can I rent a luxury yacht in Athens for a day?

Yes, luxury yachts like Sunseeker and Azimut are available for full-day charter with crew, from around €1,200.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Athens or list your own. Whether you choose a self-drive tender or a crewed motor yacht, the Saronic Gulf delivers clear water and ancient coastlines. For those with a boat to rent in Athens, consider listing your vessel on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 trips.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Athens and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
